specific_mechanism: Caldera eruption precursors include: (a) seismic swarms (changes in microquake rate and depth), (b) ground tilt + uplift, (c) CO2 / SO2 flux changes, (d) hydrothermal temperature anomalies. None is individually predictive; a multivariate, time-lagged combination is required. Machine-learning approaches identify ergodic precursor signatures that transfer across calderas
llm_application: An LLM fine-tuning protocol modeled on caldera monitoring: collect multivariate, time-lagged 'unrest signatures' from the base model (loss curves, gradient norm changes, activation drift, evaluation slip) and use them as precursor signals for capability emergence vs degradation; trigger fine-tune interventions based on the lagged signature pattern rather than a single threshold.
novelty_claim: Existing fine-tune monitoring uses single-signal thresholds (loss, eval metric). No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work formalizes a multivariate, time-lagged caldera-style precursor signature with transfer across models for triggering fine-tune interventions.
